title: Error: timed out waiting for file
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I get this error when I try a simple SQL query on my MariaDB container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

It's hard to establish a reproducible scenario in my case, but this is a fresh vanilla install of Ubuntu Server 22.04...

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: timed out waiting for file /home/mariadb/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a0a800ba0881ce593e7c40b2e2e32c40916e9044ec48aa3d152c0403900e2151/userdata/7c2210469927db4fbf6aa14ac1919128b4514f1a9277d7ee0dd340ec9285c1c1/exit/a0a800ba0881ce593e7c40b2e2e32c40916e9044ec48aa3d152c0403900e2151: internal libpod error`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I can reproduce it every time, some results are returned after the query execution but it crashes after a few seconds.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes (podman 3.4.4 is the latest version available on Ubuntu Server 22.04.1 LTS)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Azure VM - software raid 10 with an XFS filesystem (ftype=1)

comments.body:
FYI I get this error only when I launch the query from within the container through a pseudo-tty.

I just did a test with the same query launched externally and it worked perfectly so I guess there's some sort of issue when the output returned by a command is too big for the pseudo-tty. Does it make sense?
Please try with the latest podman version 4.2. I am pretty sure we already fixed this issue (at least I remember the error message) and without a reproducer I cannot confirm.
Reopen the issues if @Luap99 is mistaken.

> Please try with the latest podman version 4.2. I am pretty sure we already fixed this issue (at least I remember the error message) and without a reproducer I cannot confirm.

I would, but like I've already said in the bug's description, podman 3.4.4 is the latest available version on Ubuntu Server 22.04.
Upstream is no longer providing support for that version, so there's not much we can do. You may have better luck filing a bug against your distribution.
